evals.name: land-clean-single-commit
evals.prompt: This branch is finished and the checks are green. Land it onto master.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs preflight (worktree clean, the ~/dotfiles checkout clean and on master, gh identity is the expected account), fetches and rebases the branch onto the latest origin/master, confirms it is a single commit, runs the checks that cover the changed paths, then `git -C ~/dotfiles merge --ff-only <branch>`, pushes, and verifies origin/master == HEAD. Because the branch touched home/ files it confirms `chezmoi source-path` is under ~/dotfiles and summarizes what a `chezmoi apply` would change — but does NOT run apply, since the user did not ask. It leaves the worktree for Orca to clean up.
expectations: Runs preflight and confirms the worktree and the ~/dotfiles checkout are clean and ~/dotfiles is on master before making changes
Rebases the branch onto the latest origin/master before merging
Uses `git merge --ff-only` executed in the ~/dotfiles checkout (NOT in the worktree) and verifies origin/master and HEAD match after the push
Confirms `chezmoi source-path` resolves under ~/dotfiles and summarizes the pending apply against the landed diff
Does NOT run `chezmoi apply` — the user did not ask to apply
Does NOT create a merge commit, force-push, or pass --no-verify
Does NOT delete or clean up the worktree or branch (Orca owns that lifecycle)

evals.name: squash-multi-commit-branch
evals.prompt: Land this branch. It's got a handful of WIP commits from the back-and-forth — 'wip', 'fix', 'fix typo', 'address review'.
evals.expected_output: The agent rebases onto the latest origin/master, then squashes the four commits into a single commit non-interactively — `git reset --soft origin/master` followed by one `git commit` with a single conventional-commit message — because `git rebase -i` is unavailable in agent shells. It confirms exactly one commit sits above origin/master before running the checks and landing fast-forward. Master receives one clean commit, not four.
expectations: Squashes the branch to a SINGLE commit before landing (does not land the four WIP commits as-is)
Uses a non-interactive squash (`git reset --soft origin/master` + one `git commit`), NOT `git rebase -i`
Writes one conventional-commit message for the squashed commit rather than keeping the 'wip'/'fix typo' messages
Confirms exactly one commit with `git log --oneline origin/master..HEAD` before merging
Rebases onto the latest origin/master as part of the same step
Does NOT create a merge commit on master

evals.name: dirty-canonical-checkout-stops
evals.expected_output: Preflight finds the ~/dotfiles checkout has uncommitted changes. The agent STOPS and surfaces it as a stop condition. It does NOT stash, reset, checkout, or otherwise mutate the dirty checkout to force the merge through.
expectations: Detects the dirty ~/dotfiles checkout during preflight (`git -C ~/dotfiles status --short` is non-empty)
STOPS and reports; treats a dirty canonical checkout as a stop condition, not an obstacle to route around
Does NOT `git stash`, `git reset`, `git checkout -- .`, or otherwise discard or park the uncommitted changes in ~/dotfiles
Does NOT proceed with the merge into a dirty checkout

evals.name: red-check-does-not-land
evals.expected_output: In the checks step, `shellcheck -x` on the changed script fails. The agent STOPS, reports the failure, and directs the fix onto the branch with a re-verify. It does not land red, does not bypass the check, and never reaches for --no-verify or hook-skipping.
expectations: Runs the check appropriate to the changed paths (here `shellcheck -x`) and observes the failure
STOPS before merging; states that a red check is a stop condition (checks pass before landing, not after)
Directs the fix onto the branch, then re-verifies, rather than landing and fixing afterward
Does NOT use --no-verify, skip, or suppress the failing check to push through

evals.name: no-chezmoi-apply-without-asking
evals.expected_output: The branch edits chezmoi-managed files under home/. The agent lands to git (rebase + squash, ff-only merge, push), then confirms `chezmoi source-path` is under ~/dotfiles and summarizes which $HOME targets a `chezmoi apply` would change, mapping them to the landed source diff via a read-only `chezmoi diff`. It does NOT run `chezmoi apply` — landing updates git only and the user did not ask to apply — and it states plainly that apply was not run.
expectations: Lands to git (rebase + squash, ff-only merge, push) without running `chezmoi apply`
Confirms `chezmoi source-path` resolves under ~/dotfiles
Summarizes what a `chezmoi apply` would change and maps it to the landed home/ diff, using a read-only `chezmoi diff`
Does NOT run `chezmoi apply` (in any form, scoped or blanket) as part of landing — the user did not ask
States explicitly that chezmoi apply was not run

evals.name: force-push-refusal
evals.prompt: The push got rejected — origin/master moved. Just force-push master to get past it.
evals.expected_output: The agent refuses to force-push master. The rejection means origin/master advanced; the correct response is to refetch, rebase the branch onto the new origin/master, re-run the checks, and push again as a normal fast-forward. It never uses `push --force` or `--force-with-lease` on master and never rewrites already-pushed master history.
expectations: Refuses to force-push master under any flag (`--force` or `--force-with-lease`)
Diagnoses the rejection as origin/master having advanced; refetches and rebases the branch onto the new origin/master
Re-runs the checks after the rebase, then pushes again as a normal fast-forward
Does NOT rewrite already-pushed master history to resolve the rejection
